sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Eversource Statement on Proposed Final Decision to Approve Aquarion Sale
feed.summary: Eversource Energy issued a statement regarding the Connecticut Public Utilities Regulatory Authority's (PURA) proposed final decision to approve the sale of Aquarion Water Company. The company is considering the conditions of the decision and noted that the sale is not included in their 2026 guidance. Eversource's 2026 earnings projection of $4.80 to $4.95 per share and long-term growth rate of 5 to 7 percent through 2030 remain unchanged, assuming Aquarion remains part of Eversource.

feed.title: Horizon Technology Addresses Lawsuits Over Monroe Merger Disclosures
feed.summary: Horizon Technology Finance (HRZN) is facing class action lawsuits over alleged proxy disclosure issues related to its proposed merger with Monroe Capital Corporation, following Monroe's asset sale. Despite denying wrongdoing, Horizon has issued supplemental disclosures and is urging shareholders to approve the merger proposals at the upcoming March 13, 2026 vote. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates HRZN as Neutral due to volatile financial performance, mixed earnings outlook, and weak technical signals, counterbalanced by supportive valuation.

feed.title: Why Is SPY ETF Down 1.3% Today, 3/5/2026?
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) dropped by 1.31% on March 6, 2026, influenced by a weak February nonfarm payrolls report and rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions. Despite recent capital outflows, retail sentiment and hedge fund activity indicate a positive outlook for SPY. Analysts assign SPY a "Moderate Buy" rating with an implied upside potential of 23.51%.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) Trading Down 2.9% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) experienced a 2.9% drop in midday trading, with trading volume significantly lower than its daily average. This comes after several brokerage firms reduced their price targets for the stock, contributing to a consensus "Hold" rating, despite the company recently surpassing its quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. SMCI boasts strong fundamentals including a low price-to-earnings ratio and a high return on equity, and institutional investors hold a significant portion of its shares.

feed.title: Vertex reports data showing opioid-free recovery with Journavx for most patients after plastic surgery
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals announced strong phase 4 trial data for its non-opioid pain therapy, Journavx (suzetrigine), demonstrating opioid-free recovery for over 90% of plastic surgery patients. Used as part of multimodal pain management, the drug, which targets the NaV1.8 sodium channel, showed high patient satisfaction and mild side effects. This offers a promising alternative for acute postoperative pain, though its approximate cost of $15.50 per tablet could be a barrier without insurance.

feed.title: J Balvin remade Van Halen's 'Jump' as Coca-Cola's World Cup anthem. He says it was 'a puzzle'
feed.summary: J Balvin has reinterpreted Van Halen's "Jump" for Coca-Cola's 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem, collaborating with Travis Barker, Amber Mark, and Steve Vai. Balvin initially hesitated due to the iconic status of the original song, but aimed to create a distinct version by adding a new Spanish verse with a Brazilian funk and hip-hop influence. He emphasized adapting the song to reflect the passion of "fútbol" and hopes the track unites people, while also commenting on the potential impact of immigration policies on the World Cup event.

feed.title: Global Payments Inc Files For Two-Part, Senior Notes Offering Of Up To $1.0 Billion - SEC Filing
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. has filed for a two-part senior notes offering, aiming to raise up to $1.0 billion. This information was disclosed in an SEC filing. The announcement was reported by Refinitiv.

feed.title: Petersburg Borough Assembly approves 10-year agreement for police gear
feed.summary: The Petersburg Borough Assembly has approved a 10-year agreement with Axon Enterprise Inc. for police equipment and services, totaling nearly $379,000. This extended contract offers perks like technology refreshes and locked-in pricing, saving the borough approximately $190,000 over the decade. The agreement covers tasers, body cameras, car camera systems, software, digital evidence management, and AI-assisted report writing, aiming to enhance accountability and reduce human error.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan's Rally Is Over—But the Bull Case Isn't
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) saw an 80% rally in four months, but technical indicators suggest a pullback to the $55-$57 range is likely. Despite giving back recent gains, the long-term bull case for FCX remains strong due to increasing copper demand from EVs, data centers, and electrification, alongside its significant gold exposure. The company's restructured Grasberg mine operations, though reducing economic ownership, provide stability through 2041 and contribute meaningful cash flow.

feed.title: Duke Energy Plans to Offer $6 Billion in Shares
feed.summary: Duke Energy announced a plan to offer up to $6 billion in shares to strengthen its capital structure and fund future investments, including renewable energy projects and infrastructure upgrades. This move is expected to enhance the company's competitiveness in the renewable energy sector and market financing capabilities. Investors are advised to monitor the market reaction and potential implications for the company's financial health.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange : NYSE to Commence Delisting Proceedings Against Allurion Technologies, Inc. (ALUR)
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) announced it will begin delisting proceedings against Allurion Technologies, Inc. (ALUR) because the company failed to maintain the required average global market capitalization of at least $15,000,000 over a consecutive 30 trading day period. Trading in Allurion's common stock and warrants has been immediately suspended. Allurion Technologies has the right to appeal this decision to a Committee of the Exchange's Board of Directors.

feed.title: Truist holds Essex Property stock rating on low supply outlook
feed.summary: Truist Securities has reiterated a Hold rating on Essex Property Trust (NYSE:ESS) with a $273.00 price target, despite the stock currently trading below this at $255.13 and showing a 4.01% dividend yield. The firm's outlook suggests Essex Property may be less affected by national job growth weaknesses compared to other landlords, largely due to minimal new housing supply in its operating markets. Although two analysts have lowered earnings estimates for the upcoming period, the REIT has maintained dividend payments for 33 consecutive years, indicating long-term stability.

feed.title: Honda Recalls 65,000 SUVs For Screens That May Go Black
feed.summary: Honda is recalling 65,135 electric crossovers, including the 2024 Honda Prologue and 2024 Acura ZDX, due to software defects that can cause instrument and infotainment displays to go blank. This issue prevents drivers from seeing critical information like speed and warning lights. The recall affects vehicles built on General Motors' Ultium battery platform, and Honda will instruct owners to take their vehicles to a service center for a software update.

feed.title: Incyte’s Drug Approved In Europe For Treating Yet Another Rare Cancer
feed.summary: Incyte announced that the European Commission has approved its drug Zynyz for an additional rare cancer, specifically for adult patients with metastatic or inoperable locally recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal. This marks the drug's second approval in Europe, as it was previously approved for Merkel cell carcinoma. The approval allows Zynyz to be used in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel for this condition.

feed.title: A Look Into Northrop Grumman Inc's Price Over Earnings
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman Inc. (NYSE: NOC) stock is currently priced at $753.38, showing a slight decrease in the current session but significant gains over the past month and year. This article examines the company's price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio and compares it to the Aerospace & Defense industry average to assess whether the stock might be overvalued or undervalued. It concludes that while a lower P/E ratio can suggest undervaluation or lack of expected future growth, it should always be considered alongside other financial metrics and qualitative analyses for informed investment decisions.

feed.title: Home Bancorp 2025 10-K: $193.8M Interest Income, EPS $5.93
feed.summary: Home Bancorp reported strong 2025 results with a total interest income of $193.8 million, contributing to revenue growth and a net income of $46.1 million. The company's earnings per common share reached $5.93 basic and $5.87 diluted, driven by increased net interest income and controlled expenses. Key highlights include improved net interest income of $133.3 million and growth in noninterest income from SBA loan sales and service fees.

feed.title: Barclays Boosts CF Industries Price Target Amid Geopolitical Tensions
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for CF Industries (CF) from $100 to $120, maintaining an Overweight rating due to anticipated elevated nitrogen prices caused by geopolitical tensions. Other firms like Goldman Sachs and RBC Capital have also raised their targets, highlighting the company's strong financial health and the ongoing global nitrogen market tightness. CF Industries is well-positioned to benefit from these market dynamics and its strategic focus on low-carbon ammonia projects.

feed.title: IMAR Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:IMAR
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Innovator International Developed Power Buffer ETF March (IMAR), including its current price, key statistics, and investment strategy. It covers the fund's assets under management, expense ratio, dividend policy, and how it uses FLEX options to buffer downside risk while capping upside potential. The piece also includes technical analysis, seasonal trends, and a detailed FAQ section addressing common investor questions about ETFs and IMAR specifically.

feed.title: Hooker Furnishings declares $0.115 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Hooker Furnishings Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.115 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The company boasts a 3.37% dividend yield and a 26-year history of dividend payments. This dividend declaration follows a recent earnings miss and a cooperation agreement to appoint a new independent director to its board.

feed.title: CoStar Group confirms relocation of Dallas office
feed.summary: CoStar Group, a commercial real estate analytics firm, has confirmed it is relocating its Dallas office to Ross Tower in the Arts District. The company will occupy over 20,000 square feet on the 14th floor of the skyscraper. This move marks a significant presence for CoStar in a prominent downtown Dallas location.

feed.title: Harvard Bioscience Approves Reverse Stock Split to Regain Compliance
feed.summary: Harvard Bioscience (HBIO) shareholders approved a 1-for-10 reverse stock split on March 6, 2026, aimed at increasing the stock's per-share trading price to regain Nasdaq compliance. This action will reduce outstanding shares from approximately 44.7 million to 4.47 million, with trading on a split-adjusted basis expected to begin on March 16, 2026. Despite a "Hold" rating from analysts and concerns about financial performance, corporate actions like this split and improving cash flow are seen as partially offsetting negative factors.

feed.title: Harvard Bioscience executes 1-for-10 reverse stock split By Investing.com
feed.summary: Harvard Bioscience (NASDAQ:HBIO) is implementing a 1-for-10 reverse stock split, effective March 13, to increase its per-share trading price and regain Nasdaq listing compliance. The split will reduce outstanding shares from approximately 44.7 million to 4.47 million, with fractional shares being cashed out. Despite recent revenue decline, analysts predict profitability this year, and the company has undertaken strategic moves like consolidating manufacturing and securing a new credit facility.

feed.title: Notable Friday Option Activity: OXY, HOOD, SNDK
feed.summary: This article highlights significant options trading activity for Occidental Petroleum Corp (OXY), Robinhood Markets Inc (HOOD), and SanDisk Corp (SNDK) on a Friday. OXY saw 99,861 contracts traded, HOOD had 244,877 contracts, and SNDK experienced 130,752 contracts, representing substantial percentages of their average daily trading volumes. The report also points out specific call and put options with particularly high trading volumes for each company.

feed.title: RSI Alert: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) Now Oversold
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors NV (NXPI) has entered oversold territory with an RSI reading of 29.4, falling below the 30-point threshold that typically indicates a stock is oversold. This technical indicator suggests that the recent selling pressure might be exhausting itself, potentially presenting a buying opportunity for bullish investors. The stock traded as low as $201.11 per share on Friday.

feed.title: Two stocks flagged for study: Equifax and Neurocrine Bio review
feed.summary: BetterInvesting Magazine has named Equifax (NYSE: EFX) and Neurocrine Bio (NASDAQ: NBIX) as "Stock to Study" for its May 2026 issue. This designation encourages investors to research these companies using fundamental data rather than serving as a buy recommendation. The magazine's Editorial Advisory and Securities Review Committee highlighted the availability of fundamental data and deemed both companies worthy of further study.

feed.title: Insider Purchase: President & CEO of $CSX Buys 25,000 Shares
feed.summary: Stephen F. Angel, President & CEO of CSX, recently purchased 25,000 shares of the company stock for an estimated $1,006,750, increasing his holdings by 20.6%. This transaction is part of broader insider trading activity at CSX, which includes both purchases and sales by various executives over the last six months. The article also provides details on hedge fund activity, Q4 2025 revenue, Congressional stock trading involving CSX, and recent analyst ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: Cooper Companies posts $1,024.1M sales and $0.66 diluted EPS in 10-Q
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (COO) reported net sales of $1,024.1 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.66, with foreign exchange impacts and strong demand for contact lenses contributing to the positive results. The company saw an 8% year-over-year increase in consolidated net sales, driven by CooperVision products, while maintaining a stable gross margin of 68%. Operating income grew to $212.8 million as sales expansion outpaced operating expense increases.

feed.title: Agilent Technologies announces planned retirement of principal accounting officer
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies (NYSE: A) announced that Rodney Gonsalves, the company's Vice President, Corporate Controller, and Principal Accounting Officer, plans to retire in January 2027. His decision is not due to any disagreements with the company's financial practices, and he will assist in the transition after a successor is found. This news follows the company's recent Q1 2026 financial results, which slightly missed EPS and revenue forecasts, leading Morgan Stanley to revise estimates and lower its price target while maintaining an "Overweight" rating.

feed.title: Galapagos Launches Major 2026 Subscription Right Plan, Expanding Equity Incentive Pool
feed.summary: Galapagos NV has introduced a "Subscription Right Plan 2026," creating 1,750,000 new subscription rights for employees to acquire company shares, aiming to align employee incentives with shareholder interests. This plan builds on existing outstanding subscription rights, including a strategic option for Gilead Therapeutics to increase its stake to 29.9%, indicating potential future share dilution. The company's current share capital is €356,444,938.61, with 65,897,071 shares outstanding; analysts currently rate GLPG stock as a Buy with a $37.00 price target.

feed.title: New first-line treatment for advanced anal cancer gets EU approval
feed.summary: Incyte has announced that the European Commission (EC) has approved Zynyz (retifanlimab) in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel for the first-line treatment of advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal (SCAC). This approval is based on results from the Phase 3 POD1UM-303/InterAACT2 trial, which showed a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients receiving the Zynyz combination compared to chemotherapy alone. Zynyz is the first PD-1 immunotherapy approved in Europe for this indication in combination with platinum-based chemotherapy.

feed.title: Agilent Announces Planned Retirement of Principal Accounting Officer
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies announced that Rodney Gonsalves, Vice President, Corporate Controller, and Principal Accounting Officer, plans to retire in January 2027. The company clarified that his departure is not related to any disputes over accounting or internal controls and the extended notice period is to ensure a smooth transition. Agilent aims to reassure investors of continuity in its financial function amidst this senior leadership change.

feed.title: Capital Markets Information Services: Reviewing Our Moats for the Rise of AI
feed.summary: Morningstar equity analysts reviewed the moat ratings for several capital markets information services stocks in light of potential disruption from generative AI. While network effect-based moats are expected to remain robust, and proprietary data continues to be a strong defensive factor, the firm downgraded FactSet and SS&C to a "none" moat rating from "narrow." They maintained wide moats for Broadridge, Fair Isaac, Moody’s, MSCI, S&P Global, and Verisk, and a narrow moat for Clearwater.

feed.title: Marathon Petroleum Corp says CEO Maryann T. Mannen's 2025 total compensation was $19.0 million - SEC filing
feed.summary: Marathon Petroleum Corp announced that its CEO, Maryann T. Mannen, received a total compensation of $19.0 million in 2025, according to a recent SEC filing. The company specializes in oil refining, transportation, and distribution, reporting 3 million barrels of refined products sold per day in 2025 and owning 13 refineries and a network of 7,882 service stations in the United States.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands stock: Can this casino giant still win big for you?
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) has transformed its business, becoming an Asia-centric casino giant after selling its Las Vegas properties. The company's performance is now heavily reliant on Macau and Singapore tourism and gaming demand, offering investors exposure to the Asian market without needing international brokerage accounts. While analysts are generally constructive, potential investors should be aware of the high volatility and political/regulatory risks associated with its concentration in the Asian market.

feed.title: ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF Options Spot-On: On March 6th, 545.11K Contracts Were Traded, With 1.59 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On March 6th, the ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF (TQQQ.US) saw significant options trading, with a total volume of 545.11K contracts. Put options accounted for 50.34% and call options for 49.66% of the transactions. The open interest for TQQQ reached approximately 1.59 million contracts, representing 110.48% of the average over the past 30 trading days.

feed.title: Hostess, Peeps cupcakes hop onto shelves
feed.summary: Hostess Snacks has partnered with PEEPS to introduce new PEEPS Cupcakes for a limited time, celebrating the spring season. These cupcakes feature classic golden cake with marshmallow creme filling and bright yellow icing with the signature Hostess squiggle. They are available nationwide in an 8-count pack.

feed.title: O’Reilly Auto Announces $850 Million Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: O’Reilly Automotive has announced an offering of $850 million in 5.100% senior notes due 2036. The net proceeds of approximately $841 million will be used to refinance existing debt due in 2026, reduce its commercial paper program, and for general corporate purposes including potential share repurchases and acquisitions. While analysts rate ORLY stock a "Buy" with a $109.00 price target, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it as "Neutral" due to strong profitability and cash generation being tempered by high balance-sheet leverage and rich valuation.

feed.title: Galapagos (NASDAQ: GLPG) creates 1.75M employee subscription rights
feed.summary: Galapagos NV has announced the creation of 1,750,000 new subscription rights under a new "Subscription Right Plan 2026" for its employees and subsidiaries. Each right allows the holder to subscribe to one new Galapagos share. This initiative is part of an employee incentive program, adding to the 13,338,810 outstanding subscription rights under other employee plans and a significant right held by Gilead Therapeutics to increase its shareholding.

feed.title: STANLEY BLACK & DECKER, INC. ($SWK) Executive Chair; Former President and CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker, Inc.'s Executive Chair and former President and CEO, Donald Allan, Jr., is estimated to have received $14,485,454 in compensation in 2025, a 10.46% decrease from 2024. This information comes from a new DEF14A SEC filing. The article also details recent insider trading, government contracts, congressional stock trading, hedge fund activity, and analyst ratings/price targets for SWK stock.

feed.title: Duke Energy Corp files for common stock offering of up to $6.0 billion - SEC filing
feed.summary: Duke Energy Corp has filed for a common stock offering of up to $6.0 billion, according to a recent SEC filing. The company is one of the largest American energy groups, involved in electricity production and distribution, natural gas transportation and sale, and other power plant and real estate development. The filing was published on March 6, 2026, at 04:22 pm EST.

feed.title: Class I Briefs: CSX, NS
feed.summary: CSX celebrated the groundbreaking of a new Chick-fil-A Supply distribution center in Winter Haven, Florida, a $150 million investment expected to create 180 jobs and begin operations in 2027. Meanwhile, Norfolk Southern introduced its newest Landmark Series unit, the Altoona, featuring Automated Track Geometry Measurement Systems (ATGMS) which uses lasers and sensors to measure track geometry in real-time, enhancing safety and efficiency across its network. The article highlights both companies' commitment to improving their logistics and operational capabilities.

feed.title: Bunge jumps as soybean oil and crush economics strengthen, spotlighting Viterra synergy upside
feed.summary: Bunge Global SA (BG) stock rose 3.1% due to strengthening oilseed market conditions, particularly in soybean oil, which is improving crush economics and demand from biofuel sectors. The Viterra merger integration and anticipated synergy realization are also significantly contributing to investor confidence for the 2026 outlook. This movement is supported by broader commodity market trends and aligns with Bunge's strategic focus on integration benefits from the July 2025 merger.

feed.title: Boeing Secures Major Orders Amid Skyrocketing Growth in Aircraft Demand
feed.summary: Boeing has secured significant new orders and is experiencing optimism in the market, with its stock trading up 3.85%. Despite some operational struggles highlighted by its profitability ratios and high valuation metrics, the company's strong order books, increased deliveries, and strategic partnerships position it for future growth. Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating, anticipating continued recovery and market leadership in the aerospace industry.

feed.title: Salesforce Inc - Robin Washington To Assume Role Of Principal Accounting Officer Effective March 9, 2026 - SEC Filing
feed.summary: Salesforce Inc. has announced that Robin Washington will be taking on the role of Principal Accounting Officer, effective March 9, 2026. This information was disclosed in an SEC filing and reported by Reuters. The announcement is a key management update for the company.

feed.title: Stock Traders Purchase Large Volume of FedEx Call Options (NYSE:FDX)
feed.summary: FedEx (NYSE: FDX) experienced a significant increase in call option purchases, up 825% compared to its daily average, indicating strong bullish sentiment despite a 4.3% share price drop influenced by tariff-refund uncertainty and pre-earnings de-risking. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, topping estimates with an EPS of $4.82 and $23.47 billion in revenue, and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.45 per share. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $366.19.

feed.title: Investors Purchase High Volume of Call Options on Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY)
feed.summary: Investors showed unusually high bullish sentiment towards Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY), purchasing 120,397 call options, a 96% increase from typical daily volume, as the stock rallied to $53.98. This activity is supported by strong fundamentals including beating EPS estimates, an increased quarterly dividend, ongoing debt reduction efforts, and rising oil prices, alongside several analyst price target increases. The company also benefits from positive geopolitical sentiment, commodity-driven rallies, and strategic tailwinds including Warren Buffett's significant stake.

feed.title: Kevin Hern acquires Waters Corporation stock through Hern Family Revocable Trust
feed.summary: Representative Kevin Hern of Oklahoma's 1st congressional district acquired common stock in Waters Corporation (NYSE:WAT) through the Hern Family Revocable Trust. The acquisition, valued between $1,001 and $15,000, occurred on February 9, 2026, as a spinoff from Becton, Dickinson and Company. This transaction is part of routine financial disclosures required by the STOCK Act, ensuring transparency in the financial activities of elected officials.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Pattern Group (NASDAQ:PTRN) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reduced its price target for Pattern Group (NASDAQ:PTRN) from $21 to $17, while maintaining an "overweight" rating, indicating a potential 57% upside from its previous close. Despite this trimmed forecast, Pattern Group reported strong Q4 results that surpassed expectations and authorized a $100 million share buyback program. The stock, however, saw a significant dip to $10.80 on high volume and trades well below its 52-week high, despite generally positive analyst coverage.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Cuts GAP (NYSE:GAP) Price Target to $33.00
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has lowered its price target for GAP (NYSE:GAP) to $33.00 from $36.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. This adjustment follows GAP's mixed Q4 results, which saw shares plunge by 14.4% due to concerns over Athleta's performance, winter store closures, and margin pressures. Despite these issues, the company raised its FY2026 guidance and authorized a $1 billion share buyback, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus among analysts.

feed.title: CF Industries Stock Surges Amid Rising Nitrogen Prices and Geopolitical Factors
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings Inc. (CF) stock has surged by 4.51% due to strong financials, rising nitrogen prices, and clean energy initiatives. The company reported robust profitability with a 33.9% EBIT margin and $7.08 billion in total revenue, driven by sustained demand for nitrogen products. Strategic focus on clean energy and carbon capture, along with positive cash flow, positions CF Industries for continued growth despite long-term pricing uncertainties.
